solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public boolean isPowerOfFour(int n) {
        return n > 0 && (n & (n - 1)) == 0 && (n & 0x55555555) != 0;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <This solution uses bitwise operations to check if n is a power of four. First, it ensures n is positive and a power of two by checking (n & (n - 1)) == 0. Then, it verifies that the set bit is in an odd position using (n & 0x55555555) != 0, which is characteristic of powers of four.>
